what_it_does: This file is a posted noticeboard note in the provider-registry channel that records a corrected root-cause diagnosis: the stale-registry behavior was caused by a long-running MCP server that cached registries hours before new registry rows were created, not by agents reading a hardcoded document list.
observations: It explicitly refutes board://item-42e4b0ad, the author's earlier incorrect diagnosis.
The MCP server processes started at 2026-06-22 09:57, while new registry rows were created between 21:15 and 22:23 the same day.
Agents calling cc_board/cc_attachments through the live MCP server saw only five attachment types because the server held the old registry.
The author's direct .venv Python process, with a fresh import, saw the new rows and could use the new note item-type, images/dragnet_runs attachment types, and related tools.
The fix is an MCP reconnect/server reload, which is deferred to Tim per a connector rule.
The change is described as reversible and requiring no data change.
standouts: The author publicly corrects their own prior misattribution.
The key distinction is between on-disk/fresh-process availability and the live MCP/connector runtime surface.
The fix is an operational reload rather than a code or data change.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, state, title, author_session, channel, links, and history.
Uses board:// URIs to reference related noticeboard items.
Uses a narrative markdown body to explain diagnosis, evidence, and fix.
Links include semantic kinds such as refutes and sourced_from.
concerns: The fix depends on a specific person (Tim) and an external MCP reload, so the note itself does not resolve the issue.
No code symbols are present, so the file is purely documentary.
notes: A self-corrected incident note that pins the stale-registry symptom on a cached live MCP server and defers the fix to a connector reload.
questions: Are images.py, board_edges/reply_to.py, item_types/note.py, and dragnet_runs.py files, database rows, or registry entries?
What is the 'adjacent-possible agent' and how did it confirm the finding?
What is the 'connector rule' that makes the MCP reload Tim-only?
What do cc_board, cc_attachments, cc_images, cc_retire, and cc_dragnet refer to exactly?
What does 'fell back to signal' mean in this context?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the title and body both state it is a corrected-cause note, and the content supports that purpose.
apparent_themes: Live runtime state versus persisted disk state.
Registry caching and MCP server lifecycle.
Fresh process behavior versus long-running process behavior.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to update the shared ledger with the accurate root cause, prevent the earlier misattribution from being acted on, and communicate that the real remediation is reloading the MCP server rather than changing code or data.
novelty: It frames the problem as a 'stale live MCP server' rather than a hardcoded agent read, and it explicitly refutes the author's own prior note.
contribution: It contributes a corrected causal model and the boundary condition that new registry artifacts are on disk and work in fresh processes but are not live on the MCP surface until the server reloads, which helps a fused system avoid misdiagnosing similar symptoms.
summary_for_embedding: A provider-registry noticeboard note self-correcting a prior diagnosis, stating that a long-running MCP server cached registries before new rows were added, so agents saw stale registry data until an MCP reconnect/reload.
intention_for_embedding: To record the verified root cause and required remediation (MCP server reload) for a stale-registry incident.
